mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals sustained household income expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the zero lower bound forces investors out of safe yields and into equities, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously falling unemployment and rising payrolls signal accelerating labor market tightness and income growth, which jointly boost consumption expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the UNRATE-PAYEMS interaction term relative to 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7%, a month-over-month increase in retail sales signals that consumer demand is expanding despite labor market slack, causing upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When the trigger is met, S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and weakens near-term earnings expectations, producing negative 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When INDPRO declines month-over-month, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 1%, a rising consumer price index signals successful reflation that reduces real debt burdens and boosts nominal revenue growth, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger is met, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate larger than 0.2 percentage points signals rapidly deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering negative 20-day S&P 500 returns via downward consumption expectations.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE rises more than 0.2pp over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter change in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that lifts corporate revenues, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: When GDPC1 qoq change is positive, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.
